journal_entry.description: Purchase of machinery from Ford for factory expansion, including freight, installation, and testing costs
journal_entry.rationale: Under IFRS (IAS 16), the cost of property, plant, and equipment includes the purchase price and all costs directly attributable to bringing the asset to the location and condition necessary for it to be capable of operating in the manner intended by management. This includes freight, installation, and testing/commissioning costs. All costs ($700,000 + $30,000 + $20,000 + $50,000 = $800,000) are capitalized to Machinery. The $700,000 machinery purchase is on account (Accounts Payable increases), while the $100,000 in additional costs were paid by cheque (Cash decreases).

output.reason: The transaction involves two credit lines: (1) Accounts Payable increases by $700,000 for machinery purchased on account = liability increase; (2) Cash/Bank decreases by $100,000 ($30,000 freight + $20,000 installation + $50,000 testing) paid by cheque = asset decrease. Under IFRS, all costs necessary to bring the asset to working condition (freight, installation, testing) are capitalized to the machinery asset on the debit side, while the credits reflect the sources of payment.

credit_classifier.reason: The transaction involves two credit lines: (1) Accounts Payable increases by $700,000 for machinery purchased on account = liability increase; (2) Cash/Bank decreases by $100,000 ($30,000 freight + $20,000 installation + $50,000 testing) paid by cheque = asset decrease. Under IFRS, all costs necessary to bring the asset to working condition (freight, installation, testing) are capitalized to the machinery asset on the debit side, while the credits reflect the sources of payment.

output.reason: Under IFRS (IAS 16), machinery purchase price ($700,000) plus all costs directly attributable to bringing the asset to working condition (freight $30,000, installation $20,000, testing and commissioning $50,000) are capitalized as a single asset (Machinery/Property, Plant & Equipment). This represents 1 asset increase totaling $800,000, regardless of the multiple payment methods (on account and by cheque).
